claimText: Labeo uru and Labeo chekida are newly discovered freshwater fish species endemic to the Western Ghats river systems.
evidenceExcerpt: Both are freshwater fish endemic to the Western Ghats river systems
examUse: Statement-based question on endemism and habitat.

claimText: Labeo uru is found in the Chandragiri River, a west-flowing interstate river originating from the Pattimala hills in Karnataka.
evidenceExcerpt: Labeo uru is found in the Chandragiri River, a west-flowing interstate river
examUse: Correct-pair question matching species to their specific river habitats.

claimText: Labeo chekida inhabits the Chalakkudy river, which originates in the Anamalai Hills.
evidenceExcerpt: Labeo chekida inhabits the Chalakkudy river formed in the Anamalai Hills
examUse: Correct-pair question matching species to their specific river habitats.

claimText: The discovery of Labeo uru and Labeo chekida was made by the ICAR-National Bureau of Fish Genetic Resources (NBFGR).
evidenceExcerpt: Discovered by ICAR-National Bureau of Fish Genetic Resources (NBFGR)
examUse: Statement-based question on the discovering institution.
